title: Energy Pyramid Simulator
creator: Dan McCreary
subject: Ecology
description: Interactive pyramid visualization where students adjust producer energy input and transfer efficiency to calculate energy at each trophic level

learningObjectives: Calculate energy available at each trophic level using the ten percent rule
Explain how small changes in transfer efficiency dramatically affect top-predator energy
Compare energy, biomass, and numbers pyramids
prerequisites: Understanding of trophic levels and food chains

keyQuestions: How much energy reaches the top of a five-level food chain?
What happens to the 90% of energy that is not transferred?
Why are apex predators always rare in ecosystems?
When can a numbers pyramid be inverted?
commonMisconceptions: Students often think the 10% rule is exact rather than an average that varies from 5-20%
Students may believe energy is destroyed rather than converted to heat at each level
Students may assume all three pyramid types (energy, biomass, numbers) always look the same
assessmentOpportunities: Students complete calculation worksheets at different efficiency settings
Students solve backward-calculation problems for apex predator energy requirements
Students compare and sketch the three pyramid view types
